statement.markdown: Given an array A of size n, a leader in the array is defined as an element which is greater than all elements to its right side. The task is to find all leaders in the array. The array will contain distinct integers. The input array will be provided on the standard input, one element per line, with the first line containing the number of elements in the array, followed by each element on a new line. The output should be the leaders in the array, one per line, in the order they appear in the array.
statement.inputFormat: The first line contains a single integer n, the number of elements in the array. The next n lines contain the elements of the array, one per line.
statement.outputFormat: The output will contain the leaders in the array, one per line.
statement.notes: The array will contain distinct integers.

constraints.description: The number of elements in the array

examples.explanation: The leaders in the array are 17 and 5 because 17 is greater than 4, 3, and 5, and 5 is greater than no elements to its right (it is the last element).
The only leader in the array is 6 because it is greater than all elements to its right (there are no elements to its right).

hints.text: start by thinking about how you would find a leader in an array if you only had to look at two elements at a time. can you think of a simple algorithm for this?

once you have a simple algorithm for finding a leader in an array of two elements, think about how you can extend this algorithm to find leaders in an array of any size. can you think of a way to use a single pass through the array to find all leaders?
consider using a temporary variable to keep track of the maximum element seen so far. can you think of a way to use this variable to efficiently find all leaders in the array?

think about how you can use the temporary variable to keep track of the maximum element seen so far, and how you can use this information to find all leaders in the array. can you think of a way to do this in a single pass through the array?

the key to solving this problem is to realize that a leader in the array is an element that is greater than all elements to its right side. can you think of a way to use this insight to find all leaders in the array, using a single pass through the array and a temporary variable to keep track of the maximum element seen so far?

editorial.title: Finding Leaders in an Array
editorial.intuition: The problem requires identifying leaders in an array, which are elements that are greater than all elements to their right. The brute force approach involves comparing each element with all elements to its right, resulting in a time complexity of O(n^2). However, a more efficient approach can be achieved by iterating through the array from right to left and keeping track of the maximum element encountered so far.
editorial.bruteForce: The brute force approach is correct but slow due to its quadratic time complexity, making it inefficient for large inputs. This approach involves using nested loops to compare each element with all elements to its right.
editorial.betterSolution: A more efficient solution can be achieved by using a single pass through the array from right to left, reducing the time complexity to O(n).
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution involves iterating through the array from right to left and maintaining a variable to store the maximum element encountered so far. If an element is greater than the current maximum, it is added to the list of leaders and the maximum is updated. This approach ensures that all leaders are identified in a single pass, resulting in a time complexity of O(n) and a space complexity of O(n).
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The correctness of the optimal solution can be proven by induction, where it is shown that the maximum element encountered so far is always greater than or equal to the current element. This ensures that all leaders are identified and added to the list in the correct order.
editorial.dryRun: A dry run of the optimal solution can be performed by manually iterating through a sample array and keeping track of the maximum element encountered so far. This helps to verify the correctness of the solution and understand how it works.
commonMistakes: Using a brute force approach with nested loops, resulting in a quadratic time complexity.
Failing to update the maximum element correctly, leading to incorrect identification of leaders.
Not iterating through the array from right to left, resulting in incorrect ordering of leaders.
